note: M4 — Read (unfold) becomes the primary surface (design contract: docs/flowmap/m4-read-primary-design.md). The P1 promotion flip: the app boots into reading mode via a pure, acceptance-tested decision (resolveBootSurface — sticky surface, default read, empty model boots the editor); open()/close() record the surface under SURFACE_KEY. Map-first authoring: the fragment nodes landed in the bundle before the code, so every change is a modify against the current map. Resume: npm run flowmap:status -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/m4-read-primary.plan.json · prove behaviour: npm run flowmap:acceptance -- --plan docs/flowmap/plans/m4-read-primary.plan.json

phases.title: Pure surface contract
phases.subtitle: AppSurface + normalizeSurface + resolveBootSurface in the viewspec module — the boot decision as a testable function

phases.title: Surface transitions recorded
phases.subtitle: unfold open()/close() persist the surface choice under SURFACE_KEY

phases.title: Boot flip
phases.subtitle: main.ts resolves the boot surface after first paint and opens reading mode when it wins

intent.problem: There is no vocabulary for which surface the app is on; boot always lands in the editor and nothing can state, store or test the alternative.
intent.approach: A two-value union type in the pure viewspec contract module: 'read' | 'edit'.
intent.rationale: The surface choice is part of the view contract (M3 precedent: pure, serializable, zero imports); a union type makes every consumer exhaustive.

fm.name: AppSurface
fm.description: the two app surfaces (M4): 'read' (unfold reading mode, the primary) or 'edit' (the dense editor) — the value persisted under SURFACE_KEY and resolved at boot

intent.problem: localStorage can hold anything (corruption, older writes, user edits); an unchecked read could mis-drive boot or crash it.
intent.approach: Strict schema boundary: exactly 'read' or 'edit' pass through; every other value maps to null.
intent.rationale: Same tolerant-boundary posture as normalizeViewSpec, but strict-not-clever: nothing ever writes non-canonical values, so coercing them would only mask a corrupted store (design §2 judgment 3).

fm.name: normalizeSurface
fm.description: strict schema boundary for the stored surface: exactly 'read' or 'edit' pass through, everything else (case variants, numbers, objects, null) is null — storage garbage can never mis-drive boot

cases.name: case variant is rejected, not coerced

intent.problem: The boot-surface decision (the actual P1 flip) would otherwise live as an untestable inline conditional in main.ts.
intent.approach: One pure function of (stored, hasNodes): empty model always boots the editor; otherwise the stored surface is sticky and the default is read.
intent.rationale: Read is primary because it is the default, not because the editor is locked away (design §2 judgment 1); pure means the decision table is acceptance-proven before the wiring exists.

fm.name: resolveBootSurface
fm.description: the pure boot decision (M4 P1 flip): empty model always boots the editor; otherwise the stored surface is sticky and the default is read — read is primary because it is the default, not a cage

cases.name: fresh profile with a model boots READ — the P1 default flip
empty model always boots the editor, even with stored 'read'
storage garbage falls back to the read default

intent.problem: Nothing records that the user is on the reading surface, so a reload cannot restore it.
intent.approach: open() writes 'read' under SURFACE_KEY as part of entering the surface.
intent.rationale: open/close ARE the surface transitions; persistView may gain other call sites where a surface write would be wrong (design §2 judgment 4). Structure-only change (ctx/DOM-bound closure); behaviour is proven at runtime in a real browser.

fm.name: open
fm.description: enter the reading surface: rebuild units from ctx.state, restore the per-diagram ViewSpec, sync selection in, show the overlay — and record the surface choice ('read' under SURFACE_KEY, M4) so boot is sticky

intent.problem: Leaving reading mode must make the editor the boot surface, or the flip becomes a cage.
intent.approach: close() writes 'edit' under SURFACE_KEY as part of leaving the surface; the dock ✕ and outermost Escape both route through it.
intent.rationale: A wrapper around the returned UnfoldApi would miss every in-overlay exit (Escape chain and ✕ call the internal closure directly). Structure-only change; behaviour proven at runtime.

fm.name: close
fm.description: leave the reading surface for the editor: save the ViewSpec, hand the selection back, hide the overlay — and record the surface choice ('edit' under SURFACE_KEY, M4); called by the dock ✕ and the outermost Escape

intent.problem: Boot unconditionally lands in the dense editor; the committed P1 direction says reading mode is the primary surface.
intent.approach: After the existing first paint and history baseline, main.ts calls resolveBootSurface(localStorage[SURFACE_KEY], model non-empty) and calls unfold.open() when it returns 'read'.
intent.rationale: After-first-paint placement means the editor underneath is fully ready, so close() reveals a working editor with zero extra work; the overlay already covers the editor whenever shown. #readBtn keeps its binding as the editor→read affordance.

fm.name: first render
fm.description: render, breadcrumb, mermaid sync, baseline history, then resolve the boot surface (resolveBootSurface: sticky, default read — open the reading overlay when it wins, M4) and load optional bodies.json
